content.full_text:
Summary of Telephone Advice

No. M-91-127
Staff: Cassady, Lynda
Advisee: Purcell, Vi (441-5050)
Date: January 21, 1991
Subject: Gift Reporting

FACTS

A lobbyist and a legislator are invited to attend a social event in San Francisco. The lobbyist offered to drive the legislator to the event.

QUESTION

Is the transportation provided by the lobbyist to the legislator a gift?

ANSWER

Yes, the transportation is a gift and is subject to the $10.00 gift limitation.

ANALYSIS

After discussion with Jeanne Pritchard and Carla Wardlow, it was determined that the definition of a gift did not exempt such travel. (Section 82028.) Accordingly, the transportation would be subject to the gift prohibition. (Section 86203.)

In order to determine the fair market value of the transportation, it was suggested to the lobbyist that a standard travel reimbursement rate (i.e. 21 cents per mile) plus any parking and toll fees divided by the number of passengers be used.
